title: FINDING: client-api.linear.app rejects OAuth tokens
description:
Empirically verified 2026-02-25 via lt sync probe.

GET https://client-api.linear.app/sync/bootstrap?type=full&onlyModels=Issue
Authorization: Bearer <oauth_token>

Response: HTTP 403
{
  "error": "OAuth token authentication not allowed on this endpoint",
  "extensions": {
    "type": "forbidden",
    "code": "FORBIDDEN",
    "statusCode": 403,
    "userPresentableMessage": "OAuth token authentication not allowed on this endpoint"
  }
}

Conclusion: Linear explicitly gates client-api.linear.app to browser session auth.
OAuth tokens (including those from the documented public OAuth2 flow) are refused.
The "local-first via Linear sync protocol" approach is not viable.

Alternatives:
  1. Public GraphQL + periodic polling + local SQLite cache (recommended)
  2. Webhooks (requires reachable server, not suitable for a TUI)
  3. Personal API tokens -- likely same result since the check is on auth METHOD
     not auth credential type. Could be tested but low probability of success.
close_reason: Finding documented. Pivot to GraphQL polling + local cache.
